title: T-017 — Auto-MWO from downtime consumer (V-MNT-22, D-MNT-4) + downtime linkage Server Action
prompt:
# T-017 — Downtime → auto-MWO consumer

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §7.1 D-MNT-4, §11.6 V-MNT-22, §6.2 data flow, §9.5 (mwo.downtime_event_id)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Close CIG-3 cross-module gap (Auditor A): subscribe to 08-production `downtime.created` outbox events; when 08-production emits a downtime event with `create_maintenance_task=true` (M06 checkbox), create an MWO `state='requested'`, `source='auto_downtime'`, `downtime_event_id=<event.id>`. Also expose `linkMwoToDowntimeEvent` Server Action used by MWO Detail Downtime tab (T-022).

## Pre-flight
Verify 08-production `downtime_events` table exists and emits outbox event `downtime.created`. If 08-production §8.2.5 (T-035..T-040) has NOT landed in main yet, the consumer module is still safe to write — the dispatcher (T-012) will gracefully discard events for unknown types. Add a feature flag `mnt.auto_downtime_consumer.enabled` (default true) to short-circuit.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/worker/src/maintenance/auto-downtime-consumer.ts` registered as outbox consumer for event_type `downtime.created` in `apps/worker/cron.ts` (or event-driven runner).
2. Consumer:
   - Validates payload Zod schema (defined in `packages/events/src/downtime.ts` — if absent, import a minimal local schema and add a STUB comment requiring 08-production to publish a canonical schema).
   - If `payload.create_maintenance_task=true`: insert maintenance_work_orders row `state='requested'`, `source='auto_downtime'`, `downtime_event_id=payload.id`, `equipment_id=payload.equipment_id`, `priority=mapSeverityToPriority(payload.severity)`, `requester_user_id=payload.reporter_user_id`, `requester_reason=payload.reason`.
   - V-MNT-22: assert `downtime_event_id NOT NULL` for source='auto_downtime'.
   - Anti-dup: idempotency_key = `format('mwo:auto_downtime:%s', payload.id)` (OQ-MNT-02 anti-dup window 1h — also enforced by unique idempotency_key constraint).
3. `apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/link-downtime.ts` Server Action `linkMwoToDowntimeEvent({mwoId, downtimeEventId})`:
   - RBAC mnt.mwo.assign (manager-tier).
   - UPDATE mwo SET downtime_event_id, source='auto_downtime' (if previously manual).
   - Emit outbox `mwo.downtime_linked` event.
4. Vitest with Testcontainers: consumer creates MWO on valid payload; rejects payload without equipment_id; idempotency dedup; linkMwoToDowntimeEvent updates row and emits event.

## Files
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/auto-downtime-consumer.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/maintenance/__tests__/auto-downtime-consumer.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/link-downtime.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/__tests__/link-downtime.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a `downtime.created` outbox event with `create_maintenance_task=true`, when consumer runs, then one MWO row `state='requested'`, `source='auto_downtime'`, `downtime_event_id=payload.id` is created.
2. Given the consumer runs twice on the same outbox event (replay), when checked, then exactly one MWO exists (idempotency_key dedup).
3. Given an outbox event with `create_maintenance_task=false`, when consumer runs, then no MWO is created.
4. Given linkMwoToDowntimeEvent({mwoId, downtimeEventId}), when called, then mwo.downtime_event_id is set and outbox row `mwo.downtime_linked` is written.

## Test strategy
- RED: two vitest files asserting all four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test maintenance/auto-downtime-consumer` and `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run link-downtime`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not create MWO without idempotency_key — replay would duplicate.
- Do not bypass V-MNT-22 (`downtime_event_id NOT NULL` for source='auto_downtime').
- Do not subscribe to 08-production tables directly — use outbox events only (no cross-module DB dependency).
- Do not block the consumer on malformed payload — log + DLQ + continue.
- Do not invent a downtime schema if 08-production has not published one — use a minimal local STUB schema and escalate to 08-production owner before merging.

pipeline_inputs.description: Outbox consumer for 08-production downtime.created → auto-MWO + linkMwoToDowntimeEvent Server Action. Closes CIG-3 cross-module gap.
pipeline_inputs.details: Cross-module CIG-3 closer. Anti-dup via idempotency_key (OQ-MNT-02 anti-dup window 1h). Feature-flagged for safe rollout. Outbox events (mwo.requested auto_downtime, mwo.downtime_linked) are dispatched via foundation T-112 outbox primitive so 15-OEE MTBF/MTTR consumers receive them.
out_of_scope: Production WO consumption ⇄ Quality hold gate CIG (08-production T-PROD-QA-HOLD-GATE)
UI Downtime linkage modal (T-022 covers MNT-M-04)

Given consumer runs twice on same event (replay), when checked, then exactly one MWO exists
Given event with create_maintenance_task=false, when consumer runs, then no MWO is created
Given linkMwoToDowntimeEvent({mwoId, downtimeEventId}), when called, then mwo.downtime_event_id is set and outbox mwo.downtime_linked is written

Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test auto-downtime-consumer` and `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run link-downtime`
